response:
The engine score hits fifty when \( x = 2 \). Solving \( -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 20 = 50 \) yields \( -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 70 = 0 \). Testing values shows \( x = 2 \) works, as \( -16 + 12 + 72 - 70 = 0 \). Factoring gives \( (x-2)(-2x^2 - x + 35) = 0 \). The quadratic part has no real roots, so only two years pass before the score reaches fifty.

For scores above sixty, solve \( -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 20 > 60 \). This simplifies to \( -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 80 > 0 \). Testing values finds roots at \( x = 2.5 \) and \( x = 4 \). The inequality holds between these points. Thus, the valid range is \( 2.5 < x < 4 \).

Note: All lowercase words appear three times or fewer.
